Steps:
1. Take a clean Mac machine with no prior agent installed
2. Copy the new SSMK build agent installer .pkg to the Mac
3. Double-click the .pkg and follow the installer prompts
4. Wait for installation to complete
5. Open Web Console and check if the Mac appears under managed computers
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent installs successfully. In Web Console the Mac shows as managed with status Online. On the Mac run ""ps aux | grep dcagentservice"" in Terminal to confirm the agent service process is running

Steps:
1. Open console
2. Go to Agent > Computers and click Add Computer
3. Enter the target Mac IP/hostname and credentials
4. Click Install to push agent remotely
5. Wait for push install to complete
EXPECTED RESULT: Mac appears in Web Console as managed. On the Mac Terminal run ""ps aux | grep dcagentservice"" to confirm agent is running. No manual intervention was needed on the Mac

Steps:
1. Configure proxy in server
2. Install Mac agent on this machine using the new build
3. Open Web Console and check if the Mac appears as managed
4. On the Mac open computer.log to check proxy-related log entries
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent communicates using the proxy. In agent logs you should see no connection errors. In Web Console the Mac shows Online. If proxy auth is configured the log should NOT show ""Failed to read proxy credential from vault""

Steps:
1. Configure proxy settings on mac agent through settings.
2. Verify outbound calls are successful
3. Check logs for proxy usage
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent communicates via proxy successfully with no connection failures

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Mac Agent Install with WFA Enabled
Steps:
1. In Web Console and enable Waiting For Approval (WFA)
2. Fresh install Mac agent on a new Mac
3. In Web Console check Computers > Waiting For Approval section
4. Verify the Mac appears in the WFA list
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent installs and shows in WFA list in Web Console. Agent does NOT appear in the managed computers list yet. On the Mac open Terminal and check logs: ""cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i approval"" - should show ""Requesting server for Approval Status""

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Mac Agent Install with WFA Disabled
Steps:
1. In Web Console ensure WFA is disabled under Agent > Settings
2. Fresh install Mac agent on a new Mac
3. In Web Console check if Mac appears directly in managed computers
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent installs and appears directly as managed in Web Console without any approval step. Logs should show ""Authtoken Response Success"" indicating direct token exchange succeeded

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Mac Agent Upgrade with WFA Enabled
Steps:
1. Have a Mac agent on an older build already approved and communicating with DS
2. Enable WFA on DS
3. Upgrade the Mac agent to the new SSMK build
4. In Web Console verify the Mac is still in managed computers (not moved back to WFA)
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent upgrades successfully. The Mac remains in managed computers list and does NOT get moved to WFA queue. Agent continues communicating normally. Check Mac logs: ""cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i upgrade"" should show ""Agent Upgrade occured. Proceeding to check and handle post update actions""

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Mac Agent Upgrade with WFA Disabled
Steps:
1. Have a Mac agent on an older build
2. Ensure WFA is disabled on DS
3. Upgrade Mac agent to new build
4. Verify agent communicates normally
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent upgrades and resumes DS communication. No WFA flow triggered

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify ds-server-infoV2.xml IP Scope on Mac
Steps:
1. In console configure IP scope settings
2. On the Mac agent trigger a refresh by running "sudo /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/bin/cfgupdate" in Terminal
3. Look for ds-server-infoV2.xml getting processed in logs
EXPECTED RESULT: IP Scope gets applied correctly

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify binaries-checksumV2.json Signature Verification on Mac
Steps:
1. Upgrade Mac agent to the new SSMK build
2. On the Mac open Terminal and check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/computer.log | grep -i checksum"
3. Look for verification-related entries
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Checksum Validated Successfully"".

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Credential Manager Push Module on Mac
Steps:
1. In Web Console configure a credential for a target Mac (e.g. for software deployment)
2. Trigger a push operation (like software install) that uses this credential
4. Verify the push operation completes
EXPECTED RESULT: Push operation completes successfully on the Mac. In the logs there should be NO ""Failed to decrypt password with FRAMEWORK or master key"" error.

Steps:
1. In Web Console go to Remote Office settings
2. Move the Mac device to a different Remote Office scope
3. On the Mac wait for the agent to detect the RO change (up to one refresh cycle)
4. Check agent log
5. In Web Console verify the Mac now communicates with the new DS/RO
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent detects the RO change. Logs should show ""Remote office ID has to be changed"" followed by ""ProcessRemoteOfficeXML: Remote Office ID has been updated"". In Web Console the Mac now shows under the new Remote Office

Steps:
1. Fresh install or restart the Mac agent
2. On the Mac open Terminal and check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i cert"
3. Verify certificate is downloaded
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Cert already downloaded"" on subsequent runs or certificate download success on first run. The log should NOT show ""Error in certificate dictionary parameter"" or ""Error in response decryption""

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: APIVersion=v1.1 in Auth Requests on Mac
Steps:
1. perform fresh install
2. Check agent logs: network access
3. Inspect the logged URL
EXPECTED RESULT: The logged authPropsURL should contain ""APIVersion=v1.1"" as a parameter in the URL string

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Mac Device Moved to WFA Then Re-approved
Steps:
1. In Web Console move a managed Mac device to WFA state - by deleting authtoken
2. Wait a few seconds then approve the device again from Web Console > Waiting For Approval
3. On the Mac check agent logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i KeyShare"
4. Verify agent resumes normal communication
EXPECTED RESULT: After re-approval the Mac agent should show ""KeyShare: Initiating key share request"" in logs indicating it fetched new service keys. The agent should then resume normal communication and appear as Online in Web Console

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Mac Device Approved Before Agent Detects WFA
Steps:
1. In Web Console move Mac device to WFA -  by deleting authtoken
2. Immediately approve the device from console before the agent has checked its approval status
3. Wait for the next agent refresh cycle
4. Verify in Web Console the Mac is managed and Online
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent handles the approval seamlessly. No stuck state. Mac shows as managed and communicating in Web Console

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Service Key Download After Fresh Install
Steps:
1. Fresh install Mac agent on new SSMK build
2. Wait for agent to register, check Web Console for the Mac appearing)
3. On the Mac check logs: for "KeyShare"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show in this order: (a) ""KeyShare: Initiating key share request for X key(s)"" (b) ""KeyShare: Sending request to <servlet_url>"" (c) ""KeyShare: Received response of X byte(s)"" (d) ""KeyShare response: ..."" with no errors. There should be NO ""KeyShare: Failed to persist shared keys"" or ""KeyShare: response Decryption failed"" errors

Steps:
1. Have a Mac agent on pre-SSMK old build fully communicating with DS
2. Upgrade to the new SSMK build
3. Wait for agent to complete post-upgrade sync
3. On the Mac check logs: for "KeyShare"
EXPECTED RESULT: After upgrade the agent should download service keys. Logs show ""KeyShare: Initiating key share request"" and ""KeyShare: Received response of X byte(s)"". No ""KeyShare: Failed to persist shared keys"" errors

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Auth Token Migration from Plist to Vault on Upgrade
Steps:
1. Have Mac agent on old build (tokens stored in plist) fully communicating with DS
2. Upgrade Mac agent to new SSMK build
3. Wait for upgrade to complete
4. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i 'Token migration'"
5. Verify agent still communicates with DS (check Web Console - Mac should be Online)
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Token migration:"" messages. There should be NO ""Token migration: failed to open vault"" errors. If a token was not present it should show ""Token migration: <tokenName> not present in server info - skipping"" at info level (this is normal). After migration agent communicates with DS successfully

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Auth Token Storage After Fresh Install
Steps:
1. Fresh install Mac agent on new SSMK build
2. Wait for agent to complete registration with DS
3. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i 'Authtoken'"
4. In Web Console verify the Mac is Online and managed
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Fetching Auth token"" followed by ""AuthProps POST succeeded"" and ""Authtoken Response Success"". The Mac should show Online in Web Console. There should be NO ""Auth properties missing"" or ""Authtoken Response error"" in the logs

Steps:
1. Have a registered Mac agent on new build
2. From Web Console trigger an action that requires the agent to present its approval token - ex. cloud ondemand 
3. On Mac check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i 'Approval'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Approval Token set"" indicating the approval token was found and ready. There should be NO ""Approval Token pair missing"" or ""Unable to get approval token"" errors. The triggered action should complete successfully

Steps:
1. On old build Mac agent configure proxy with username and password in Web Console
2. Verify agent communicates through proxy on old build
3. Upgrade Mac agent to new SSMK build
4. After upgrade verify agent still communicates through proxy
5. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i 'Proxy credential'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Proxy credential migrated to vault successfully"" followed later by ""ProxyCredentialMigration: PROXYPASSWORD removed from plist"". Agent should continue communicating through proxy. There should be NO ""Failed to decrypt proxy password; skipping migration"" error

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Proxy Communication After Fresh Install on New Build
Steps:
1. Configure proxy settings in Web Console for the target Mac
2. Fresh install new SSMK build agent on the Mac
3. Verify Mac appears in Web Console as managed
4. From Web Console trigger a config push to the Mac
5. Check that the operation succeeds
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent communicates through proxy. Config push succeeds. There should be no ""Failed to read proxy credential from vault"" in dcondemand logs (check: ""cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i proxy"")

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Credential Decryption After Upgrade - Old Credentials Fallback
Steps:
1. On old build Mac agent configure credentials for a software deployment via Web Console
2. Run the deployment to verify credentials work on old build
3. Upgrade Mac agent to new SSMK build
4. Without re-configuring credentials trigger the same deployment again from Web Console
5. Check if deployment succeeds on the Mac
EXPECTED RESULT: Deployment should succeed using the old stored credentials. In Mac logs ("cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcconfig.log | grep -i credential") you should see "FRAMEWORK decryption failed, attempting master key fallback" (this is expected for old credentials) followed by successful credential retrieval. There should be NO "Failed to decrypt password with FRAMEWORK or master key" which would indicate total failure

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Credential Decryption on New Build - FRAMEWORK Key
Steps:
1. On Mac agent running the new SSMK build go to Web Console and configure a new credential for deployment
2. Trigger a software deployment using this new credential
3. Verify deployment succeeds on the Mac
4. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcconfig.log | grep -i credential"
EXPECTED RESULT: Deployment succeeds. without any ""FRAMEWORK decryption failed"" or ""Failed to decrypt password"" errors since the credential was created with the new FRAMEWORK key

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Key Rotation - Server Rotates Service Module Key
Steps:
1. Have a working Mac agent on new build
2. From the server side trigger a key rotation for a service module (this is a server admin action)
3. Wait for the Mac agent to perform its next sync cycle (or force a refresh: "sudo /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/bin/dcconfig refresh")
4. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i KeyShare"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show the agent detected a new UUID and initiated a key share request. Should see ""KeyShare: Initiating key share request for X key(s)"" and ""KeyShare: Received response of X byte(s)"". After key rotation any operation that uses the rotated module key should still work

Steps:
1. Have a working Mac agent on new build
2. Server triggers key rotation with the adaption flag (key at adaption stage meaning UUID-only rotation)
3. Wait for agent sync
4. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i 'transition\|adaption'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show "KeyShare: Module <moduleName> is in transition, retaining existing key under new UUID". This means the agent kept the existing key under the new UUID without fetching a new key from the servlet. No "KeyShare: Incomplete key information" errors should appear

Steps:
1. Have a Mac agent on new build where only certain modules are active
2. Ensure the server has UUIDs for modules not installed on this Mac (other than SOM / FRAMEWORK)
3. Trigger agent sync
4. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i 'not mapped'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show "KeyShare: UUID <uuid> is not mapped to a local module, skipping" for UUIDs that do not correspond to modules active on this Mac. The agent should NOT make servlet calls for these skipped UUIDs

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: UUID Timestamp Not Committed on Key Fetch Failure
Steps:
1. Install Mac agent on new build
2. Before the agent can reach the keyshareServlet block network access to the server (e.g. disconnect WiFi or block the port)
3. Wait for the agent to attempt key download
4. Restore network access
5. Wait for next sync cycle
6. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i KeyShare"
EXPECTED RESULT: During the blocked period logs should show "Error fetching Key share response" or similar error. After network is restored the agent should retry and eventually show "KeyShare: Received response of X byte(s)". The key point is the agent retried - if timestamps were committed prematurely it would have skipped the UUIDs showing "UUID <uuid> is up to date, skipping" which should NOT appear before the keys are successfully stored

Steps:
1. Upgrade Mac agent from pre-SSMK build to new build
2. Wait for post-upgrade sync
3. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcconfig.log | grep -i 'service-master-keys\|KeyShare\|validateServiceKeys'"
EXPECTED RESULT: After upgrade the agent should validate service keys. If keys are missing or invalid the agent re-downloads them. Logs should eventually show successful key storage with no persistent ""KeyShare: Failed to persist shared keys"" errors

Steps:
1. Trigger a client certificate request on the Mac agent (this happens during initial install)
2. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcagentservice.log | grep -i CSR"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Proceeding to request CSR..."" followed by ""Encrypting CSR requesting"" then ""Request encrypted"". There should be NO ""Error in encryption"" (critical) or ""Error in CSR generation"" (critical). After the server responds logs should show ""Proceeding to decrypt response"" and ""Certificate import successful""

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Binary Checksum ECDSA Verification on Mac
Steps:
1. Upgrade Mac agent to new build
2. Wait for upgrade to complete
3. Check upgrader logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/macAgentUpgrader.log | grep -i checksum"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Agent Checksum validation succeeded"". There should be NO ""Agent Checksum validation failed"" or ""ECDSA signature verification failed for binaries-checksumV2.json"" or ""Error parsing binaries-checksumV2.json structure"" errors

Steps:
1. Before upgrading the Mac agent manually tamper with the binaries-checksumV2.json file on the server or intercept and modify it
2. Trigger agent upgrade
3. Check upgrader logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/macAgentUpgrader.log | grep -i checksum"
EXPECTED RESULT: Upgrade should FAIL. Logs should show ""ECDSA signature verification failed for binaries-checksumV2.json"" or ""Agent Checksum validation failed"". The agent should NOT proceed with installing tampered binaries

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Upgrader Copies Public Key Before Checksum Verification
Steps:
1. Trigger Mac agent upgrade from Web Console
2. Check upgrader logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/macAgentUpgrader.log | grep -i 'public key\|Public key'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show either ""Public key already present in serverinfo handler"" or ""Public key copied from downloaded serverinfo plist to serverinfo handler"". This must appear BEFORE any checksum validation log entries. There should be NO ""Public key not found in downloaded serverinfo plist either"" error

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: RO Movement Uses V2 Server Info XML
Steps:
1. In Web Console move a Mac agent to a different Remote Office
2. Wait for agent to process the RO change
3. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcconfig.log | grep -i 'ProcessRemoteOfficeXML\|Remote office'"
4. Verify agent communicates with the new DS
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""ProcessRemoteOfficeXML"" entries processing the V2 XML. Should show ""Remote office ID has to be changed"" followed by ""Remote Office ID has been updated"". Agent should then communicate with the new DS (verify Mac shows Online in Web Console under new RO)

Steps:
1. In Web Console move Mac agent to a different Remote Office
2. Wait for agent to complete the RO transition
3. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcconfig.log | grep -i 'ApprovalKey\|force'"
4. Verify agent authenticates with the new DS
EXPECTED RESULT: During RO movement logs should show ""ApprovalKey force-updated in vault"" (or similar force-store messages). There should be NO ""ApprovalKey decryption failed"" (critical) errors. After the move agent should authenticate with the new DS successfully (Web Console shows Mac Online under new RO)

Steps:
1. Upgrade Mac agent to new SSMK build and let migration complete
2. Restart the agent service: "sudo launchctl stop com.manageengine.dcagentservice && sudo launchctl start com.manageengine.dcagentservice"
3. Check logs if migration gets skipped.
EXPECTED RESULT: After restart the migration check should show ""Token migration: vault already populated - skipping"" indicating it detected tokens are already in the vault and did not attempt to re-migrate them

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Full Upgrade Preserves All Auth State
Steps:
1. Have a Mac agent on old pre-SSMK build that is fully enrolled communicating with DS and has proxy configured
2. Upgrade to new SSMK build
3. After upgrade wait for agent to sync
4. In Web Console verify Mac is Online
5. Trigger an on-demand scan from Web Console
6. Trigger a software deployment that uses credentials
EXPECTED RESULT: After upgrade: Mac remains Online in Web Console without re-enrollment. On-demand scan executes successfully. Software deployment using stored credentials succeeds. Proxy communication continues working. Check logs for ""Agent Upgrade occured. Proceeding to check and handle post update actions"" and ""Migrating to vault"" and ""Token migration"" entries confirming migration ran

Steps:
1. Install or upgrade Mac agent to new build - Cloud
2. On the Mac verify dcondemand is running: "ps aux | grep dcondemand"
3. Ensure ondemand connectivity
EXPECTED RESULT: dcondemand process should be running. Logs should show ""Starting WebSocket connection to <host>:<port>"" followed by ""DMS authentication successful - UID: <partial_uid>***"" and ""Auth success - starting background tasks"". There should be NO ""Failed to load configuration"" or ""Failed to generate auth token"" errors

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent running with dcondemand connected
2. Temporarily disconnect the Mac from the network (turn off WiFi or unplug Ethernet)
3. Wait 30 seconds then reconnect the network
4. Ensure ondemand connectivity
EXPECTED RESULT: After network restore logs should show the agent retrying connections: ""Trying DC <host> with proxy mode: <mode>..."" and eventually ""DMS authentication successful"". The on-demand scan triggered from Web Console should execute on the Mac

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent connected to primary DC via dcondemand
2. Make the primary DC unreachable (e.g. block its IP on the Mac firewall or shut down primary DC)
3. Wait for dcondemand to detect the failure and failover
4. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'failed\|Trying\|next DC\|proxy mode'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show connection failure to primary DC followed by "All proxy modes failed for <primary_host>, trying next DC" and then "Trying DC <secondary_host> with proxy mode: <mode>..." eventually followed by "DMS authentication successful" on the secondary DC

Steps:
1. Install Mac Agent in OP setup
2. Ensure ondemand connectivity
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show "TCPEndpoint: server address = <hostname>, port = <port>, TLS = <true/false>" and "Connection started." for TCP path. If a notification is sent logs should show "Received new message" and "Acknowledgement Received."

Steps:
1. Configure a server-level proxy for the Mac agent in Web Console
2. Start Mac agent and verify dcondemand connects through server proxy
3. Remove the server proxy setting and restart the agent
4. Verify dcondemand falls back to system proxy (if configured) or direct connection
5. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'proxy mode'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show the proxy mode being used. When server proxy is configured: ""Trying DC <host> with proxy mode: serverProxy"". When server proxy is removed: agent should fall back to ""systemProxy"" or ""noProxy"". There should be NO ""Server proxy enabled but port invalid"" errors if proxy was configured correctly

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent running with dcondemand connected
2. From Web Console go to the Mac computer entry
3. Click Scan Now or trigger Inventory Collection
4. Wait for collection to complete
5. Check dcondemand logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'collection\|dcconfig'"
EXPECTED RESULT: On the Mac side logs should show ""Inside InitiateCollectionTask Method"" followed by ""dcconfig returned: 0"" (return code 0 means success). In Web Console the inventory data for the Mac should be updated with fresh information

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent running
2. From Web Console trigger a patch scan on the Mac
3. Wait for scan to complete
4. Check dcondemand logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'PatchScan\|patch'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show "Inside InitiatePatchScan" with task ID and "End InitiatePatchScan, retCode: 0". In Web Console the patch status for the Mac should update

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent running
2. From Web Console trigger a remote shutdown on the Mac
3. Observe Mac behavior
4. Before shutdown check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'shutdown\|power'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Inside InitiateShutdownTask Method"" and ""Shutdown/Restart task called"" with the action details. The Mac should shut down (or restart depending on the action chosen). There should be NO ""RemoteShutdownTaskDetails is empty"" or ""Missing or invalid operation"" errors

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: On-Demand Remote Desktop Session on Mac
Steps:
1. Have Mac agent running
2. From Web Console initiate a Remote Desktop session to the Mac
3. Wait for RDS to initialize
4. Check dcondemand logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'RDS\|remote'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Request for RDS services initiated..."" and eventually ""-----Native RDS started-----"". The remote desktop window should open. If the user prompt is configured logs may show prompt-related entries. There should be NO ""Failed to initialize RDSUtil"" errors

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: On-Demand Remote Office Move via On-Demand
Steps:
1. From Web Console trigger a Move to Remote Office action for the Mac
2. Wait for agent to process
3. Check dcondemand logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'MoveToRemoteOffice\|Remote Office'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Inside MoveToRemoteOffice method"" and ""Current Remote Office: <old_id>"" and ""Target Remote Office: <new_id>"" followed by ""Executed dcconfig with return code: 0"" and ""Changed RemoteOfficeName: <new_name>"". There should be NO ""Required parameters to change are null or zero"" errors

Steps:
1. On Mac agent with new build check if ondemandHelper binary exists: "ls /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/bin/ondemandHelper" (it should NOT exist)
2. Verify dcondemand is running: "ps aux | grep dcondemand"
3. Trigger any on-demand action from Web Console (scan or collection or shutdown)
4. Verify the action completes
EXPECTED RESULT: ondemandHelper binary should NOT exist on the new build. dcondemand should be running on its own. The triggered action should complete successfully proving dcondemand works independently

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent dcondemand connect to DMS server over TLS
2. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'trust\|TLS\|certificate'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Evaluating server trust"" followed by ""Trust evaluation success"". There should be NO ""Trust evaluation failed"" or ""Failed to get server trust object"" errors

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent dcondemand connected and idle for several minutes
2. Check logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'ping\|pong'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show periodic ""Ping #<number>"" entries followed by ""Pong received"" entries. There should be NO ""Ping timeout - no pong received after ping #<number>"" errors which would indicate connection issues

Steps:
1. From Web Console trigger agent uninstall on Mac
2. Check dcondemand logs before uninstall happens: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'uninstall'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Invoke ondemand uninstall"". The agent uninstall should proceed. After completion the agent processes should no longer be running on the Mac

Steps:
1. From Web Console trigger a support log upload for the Mac agent
2. Wait for upload to complete
3. Check dcondemand logs: "cat /Library/ManageEngine/UEMS_Agent/logs/dcondemand.log | grep -i 'SupportLogUpload\|log upload'"
EXPECTED RESULT: Logs should show ""Inside processSupportLogUploadTask"" followed by ""File Write Completed"". There should be NO ""Could not get component ID"" or ""componentID is not applicable"" errors

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Full Lifecycle - Fresh Install to On-Demand
Steps:
1. Take a clean Mac
2. Fresh install new SSMK build agent
3. In Web Console verify Mac appears as managed
4. Wait 2-3 minutes for full sync (keys + tokens)
5. From Web Console trigger an on-demand inventory scan
6. Verify scan data appears in Web Console
EXPECTED RESULT: Mac registers successfully. Service keys are downloaded (check logs for ""KeyShare: Received response""). Auth tokens are stored (check logs for ""Authtoken Response Success""). On-demand scan completes (check logs for ""dcconfig returned: 0""). Web Console shows updated inventory

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Full Lifecycle - Upgrade from Pre-SSMK Build
Steps:
1. Start with Mac agent on old pre-SSMK build fully enrolled with proxy and credentials configured
2. Upgrade to new SSMK build
3. Verify Mac stays Online in Web Console
4. Trigger on-demand scan - verify it works
5. Trigger software deployment using stored credentials - verify it works
6. Verify proxy communication still works
EXPECTED RESULT: All operations succeed post-upgrade. Logs show token migration ("Token migration" entries), proxy migration ("Proxy credential migrated to vault successfully"), service key download ("KeyShare: Received response"), and successful operations. No re-enrollment needed

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent on new build fully operational
2. From server side trigger a service key rotation
3. Wait for agent to sync (or force refresh)
4. From Web Console trigger a configuration push that requires module encryption
5. Verify the push succeeds
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent picks up new key (logs show ""KeyShare: Initiating key share request"" and ""KeyShare: Received response""). The configuration push using the rotated key succeeds. No ""Failed to encrypt for <module>"" or ""Failed to decrypt for <module>"" errors in logs

Steps:
1. Have Mac agent on new build communicating with DS1
2. In Web Console move the Mac to a Remote Office served by DS2
3. Wait for agent to process the move
4. Verify Mac appears Online under the new RO in DS2 console
